sections.paragraph: f. constr. תֹּועֲבַת (from the root תָּעַב), an abomination, something abominable. Pro 21:27 28:9 , תֹּועֲבַת יְהֹוָה “things which are an abomination to Jehovah.” Pro 3:32 11:1 , 20 Pro 11:20. Especially used of things which are made impure and illicit by the decrees of religion. Gen 43:32, “for it is an abominable thing to the Egyptians” (to eat with the Hebrews). Gen 46:34 Deu 14:3 specially used of things belonging to the worship of idols, 1Ki 14:24 2Ki 16:3 21:2 Ezr 9:1 Eze 16:2 and of idols themselves, 2Ki 23:13. See שֶׁקֶץ , שִׁקּוּץ .
